sections.title: Talking to him after claiming a chart once

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Um... Are you sure? I think you've got one stored somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost my chart.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's not a problem, I've got lots of copies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: You don't have enough space for the chart. Come back to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Certainly. It's not difficult.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Lots of clues have degrees and minutes written on them. These are the coordinates of the place where the treasure is buried.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I was...

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Welcome to the magnificent wonder of the Observatory, where wonder is all around you, where the stars can be clutched from the heavens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, nice intro.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Why, thanks! How might I help you?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Lost? It must have been those pesky goblins that led you astray.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Head north-east to find the city of Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I'll find the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for all your help.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: No problem at all. Come and visit again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An Observatory?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Of course. We have a superb telescope up in the Observatory, on the hill.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: A truly marvellous invention, the likes of which you'll never behold again.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well, it would be if it worked.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Don't interrupt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? It doesn't work?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no. Don't listen to him, he's joking. Aren't you, my FAITHFUL assistant?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Nope, dead serious. Hasn't been working for a long time.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Arghhh! Get back to work and stop sticking your nose in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, it's broken. How come?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, I suppose there's no use keeping it secret. Did you see those houses outside?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Up on the hill? Yes, I've seen them.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It's a horde of goblins.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Since they moved here they have caused nothing but trouble.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Last week, my telescope was tampered with.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Now, parts need replacing before it can be used again. They've even been messing around in the dungeons under this area. Something needs to be done.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Strikes me that this visitor could help us.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Stop being so rude.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Although, he has a point. What do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds interesting, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, thanks so much.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I shall need some materials for the telescope, so it can be used again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Let's start with three planks of wood for the telescope base. My assistant will help with obtaining these, won't you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: As if I don't have enough work to do. Seems I don't have a choice.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Go talk to him if you need some advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I don't have time for that.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh dear. I really do need some help.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: If you see anyone who can help then please send them my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just passing through.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Fair enough. Not everyone is interested in this place, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Welcome back! How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you teach me to solve treasure trail clues?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Ah, I get asked about treasure trails all the time! Listen carefully and I shall tell you what I know...

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Lots of clues have degrees and minutes written on them. These are the coordinates of the place where the treasure is buried.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: You have to walk to the correct spot, so that your coordinates are exactly the same as the values written on the clue scroll.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: To do this, you must use a sextant, a watch and a chart to find your own coordinates.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Once you know the coordinates of the place where you are, you know which way you have to walk to get to the place where the treasure is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiight. So where do I get those items from?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I think Murphy, the owner of the fishing trawler moored south of Ardougne, might be able to spare you a sextant. Then the nearest Clock Tower is south of Ardougne - you could probably get a watch there. I've

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: got plenty of charts myself, here have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: You don't have enough space for the chart. Come back to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Welcome back.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Thanks for all your help with the telescope. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Not right now,

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: but the stars may hold a secret for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it true your name is Mambo-duna-roona?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: How do you know tha-

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I mean, of course not, what a silly idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, thanks.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Okay, no problem. See you again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: What do you see now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see a constellation through the telescope. It looks like Scorpio.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Scorpio? Interesting. How very fitting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Scorpius, the founder of all we know relating to astronomy. There's a book about him in the reception. Perhaps you should check it out, you might learn something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then perhaps I shall.

sections.title: Using any item on the professor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of this!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Wonderful. You must be very proud to have it.

sections.title: Using any noted item on the professor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of this?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I'd rather you show me the actual item, as opposed to the note form.
